evals.description: Trigger test: skill activates for sprint planning question
evals.prompt: How should I run sprint planning for my team's upcoming 2-week sprint?

evals.description: Trigger test: skill activates for estimation question
evals.prompt: What's the best way to estimate user stories with my dev team?

evals.description: Trigger test: skill activates for Kanban setup
evals.prompt: I want to set up a Kanban board for my engineering team of 6 people

evals.description: Core task: can produce a sprint planning agenda
evals.prompt: Create a sprint planning agenda for a 2-week sprint with a team of 5 developers

evals.description: Core task: can explain story point estimation with reference table
evals.prompt: Explain story point estimation using the Fibonacci scale with examples for each level

evals.description: Core task: can design a retrospective session
evals.prompt: Design a 45-minute retrospective for my team after a rough sprint where we only completed 60% of committed work

evals.description: Core task: can recommend velocity tracking metrics
evals.prompt: What metrics should I track each sprint to monitor my team's health and velocity?

evals.description: Core task: can write a well-structured user story
evals.prompt: Write a user story with acceptance criteria for a password reset feature

evals.description: Gotcha: warns against using velocity to compare teams
evals.prompt: Team A has velocity 45 and Team B has velocity 30. How do I make Team B as productive as Team A?

assertions.value: Team B should increase their velocity to match

evals.prompt: We're behind on our deliverables this sprint. Should we skip the retrospective to save time?

evals.description: Gotcha: correctly handles WIP limit violations
evals.prompt: All our Kanban columns are full but I have a new urgent feature to start. What should I do?

evals.description: Anti-hallucination: does not invent Scrum roles that don't exist
evals.prompt: What are the roles in a Scrum team?

evals.description: Anti-hallucination: does not confuse story points with hours
evals.prompt: A 5-point story should take about 5 hours, right?

evals.description: References load: correctly points to estimation reference for deep dive
evals.prompt: I want to learn about all the different estimation techniques beyond story points - T-shirt sizing, affinity estimation, and #NoEstimates
